all_cases.description: Public names import from the single module, including resolve_metadata, and exception classes inherit from ValueError.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version, InvalidVersion, SpecifierSet, InvalidSpecifier, Requirement, InvalidRequirement, Marker, InvalidMarker, UndefinedEnvironmentName, default_environment, is_requirement_satisfied, resolve_metadata
print(Version.__name__, SpecifierSet.__name__, Requirement.__name__, Marker.__name__, resolve_metadata.__name__)
print(issubclass(InvalidVersion, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidSpecifier, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidRequirement, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidMarker, ValueError), issubclass(UndefinedEnvironmentName, ValueError))
all_cases.expected_output:
Version SpecifierSet Requirement Marker resolve_metadata
True True True True True
all_cases.actual:
Version SpecifierSet Requirement Marker resolve_metadata
True True True True True
all_cases.expected:
Version SpecifierSet Requirement Marker resolve_metadata
True True True True True

all_cases.description: Version canonicalizes release zeros, epoch, pre-release aliases, post-release aliases, and local labels.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version
vals = ['1.0.0', '2!01.02.0', '1.0ALPHA2', '1.0-r3', '1.0+LOCAL_build-5']
print([str(Version(v)) for v in vals])
print(Version('1.0') == Version('1.0.0'))

all_cases.description: Version ordering handles dev, pre, final, and post releases.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version
ordered = [Version(v) for v in ['1.0.dev1', '1.0a1', '1.0b1', '1.0rc1', '1.0', '1.0.post1']]
print(all(a < b for a, b in zip(ordered, ordered[1:])))
print([str(v) for v in sorted(reversed(ordered))])

all_cases.description: Version ordering includes epochs and local labels after public-version equality.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version
print(Version('1!0.9') > Version('2.0'))
print(Version('1.0+abc') < Version('1.0+1'))
print(Version('1.0+1') < Version('1.0+2'))

all_cases.description: Invalid version text raises InvalidVersion.
SpecifierSet applies inclusive and exclusive range clauses.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s = SpecifierSet('>=1.2, <2.0')
print(str(s))
print([s.contains(v) for v in ['1.1', '1.2', '1.9', '2.0']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet supports equality, inequality, and wildcard release prefixes.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
print([SpecifierSet('==1.2.*').contains(v) for v in ['1.2', '1.2.5', '1.3']])
print([SpecifierSet('!=1.2.*').contains(v) for v in ['1.1', '1.2.9', '1.3']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet supports compatible release bounds.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s1 = SpecifierSet('~=1.4')
s2 = SpecifierSet('~=1.4.5')
print([s1.contains(v) for v in ['1.4', '1.9', '2.0']])
print([s2.contains(v) for v in ['1.4.5', '1.4.9', '1.5']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet excludes pre-releases by default and includes them when requested.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s = SpecifierSet('>=1.0')
print(s.contains('1.1a1'))
print(s.contains('1.1a1', prereleases=True))
print(s.contains('1.1.dev1', prereleases=False))

all_cases.description: Invalid specifier text raises InvalidSpecifier.
Requirement parses normalized names, sorted normalized extras, and canonical specifiers.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
r = Requirement('My_Pkg[PDF,security-extra] >=1.0, <2')
print(r.name)
print(sorted(r.extras))
print(str(r.specifier))
print(str(r))

all_cases.description: Requirement parses direct URLs and marker text as distinct fields.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
r = Requirement('Demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl ; python_version >= "3.10"')
print(r.name)
print(r.url)
print(str(r.specifier))
print(type(r.marker).__name__)
print(str(r))

all_cases.description: Requirement equality and hashing use normalized names and sorted extras.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
a = Requirement('Pkg_Name[B,A]>=1')
b = Requirement('pkg-name[a,b] >= 1.0.0')
print(a == b)
print(len({a, b}))
print(str(b))

all_cases.description: Invalid requirement strings raise InvalidRequirement.
Marker evaluates equality and version comparisons against a caller environment.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
env = {'python_version': '3.11', 'os_name': 'posix'}
print(Marker('python_version >= "3.8"').evaluate(env))
print(Marker('os_name == "nt"').evaluate(env))

all_cases.description: Marker honors boolean precedence and parentheses.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
env = {'python_version': '3.9', 'sys_platform': 'linux', 'os_name': 'posix'}
print(Marker('python_version >= "3.8" and sys_platform == "linux"').evaluate(env))
print(Marker('python_version < "3.8" and (sys_platform == "linux" or os_name == "posix")').evaluate(env))

all_cases.description: Marker evaluates membership operators and requested extras.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
print(Marker('sys_platform in "linux darwin"').evaluate({'sys_platform': 'linux'}))
print(Marker('extra == "security-extra"').evaluate({}, requested_extras={'Security_Extra'}))
print(Marker('extra == "docs"').evaluate({}, requested_extras={'Security_Extra'}))

all_cases.description: Marker syntax errors and missing environment variables use the documented exceptions.
resolve_metadata exposes the documented projection keys and a simple semantic schema without mutating caller metadata.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import resolve_metadata
candidates = [
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['dep>=1; extra == "pdf"']},
    {'name': 'dep', 'version': '1.0'},
]
before = repr(candidates)
res = resolve_metadata(['App[PDF]>=1'], candidates)
print(sorted(res))
print(sorted(res['selected'].items()))
print(sorted((e['parent'], e['name'], tuple(e['extras'])) for e in res['edges']))
print(sorted((k, tuple(v)) for k, v in res['requested_extras'].items()))
print(sorted((r['source'], r['parent'], r['name'], tuple(r['extras'])) for r in res['requirements']))
print(repr(candidates) == before)

all_cases.description: Candidate-owned resolve_metadata preserves selected versions, semantic dependency edges, requested extras, and active requirements under candidate-order permutation.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import resolve_metadata

def edge_key(e):
    return (e['parent'], e['name'], tuple(e.get('extras') or ()), e.get('url'), e.get('specifier'), tuple((v, bool(ok)) for v, ok in e.get('specifier_matches', ())))

def req_key(r):
    return (r['source'], r['parent'], r['name'], tuple(r.get('extras') or ()), r.get('specifier'), r.get('url'), bool(r.get('marker_applicable')))

def projection_equal(a, b):
    return (
        a['selected'] == b['selected'] and
        sorted(edge_key(e) for e in a['edges']) == sorted(edge_key(e) for e in b['edges']) and
        a['dependents'] == b['dependents'] and
        a['excluded'] == b['excluded'] and
        a['requested_extras'] == b['requested_extras'] and
        sorted(req_key(r) for r in a['requirements']) == sorted(req_key(r) for r in b['requirements'])
    )

candidates = [
    {'name': 'Core_Lib', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'core-lib', 'version': '2.0'},
    {'name': 'core-lib', 'version': '3.0'},
    {'name': 'Plugin_Fast', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['ssl-lib>=2; extra == "speed"']},
    {'name': 'ssl-lib', 'version': '2.0'},
    {'name': 'App_Main', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['Plugin_Fast[Speed] @ https://example.test/plugin-fast-1.0.whl ; sys_platform == "linux" and extra == "accelerated"', 'core-lib>=1,<3']},
    {'name': 'app-main', 'version': '2.0', 'requires': ['Plugin_Fast[Speed] @ https://example.test/plugin-fast-2.0.whl ; sys_platform == "linux" and extra == "accelerated"', 'core_lib>=2,<3']},
]
env = {'python_version': '3.11', 'sys_platform': 'linux'}
root = 'App-Main[Accelerated]>=1; python_version >= "3.10"'
a = resolve_metadata([root], candidates, env)
b = resolve_metadata([root], list(reversed(candidates)), env)
url_edges = sorted((e['parent'], e['name'], tuple(e['extras']), e['url']) for e in a['edges'] if e.get('url'))
print(sorted(a['selected'].items()))
print(url_edges)
print(sorted((e['parent'], e['name']) for e in a['edges']))
print(sorted((k, tuple(v)) for k, v in a['requested_extras'].items()))
print(projection_equal(a, b))

all_cases.description: resolve_metadata uses the same public requirement fields, marker applicability, requested extras, specifier membership, and satisfaction facts for equivalent metadata variants.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, is_requirement_satisfied, resolve_metadata

def req_fields(res):
    return sorted((r['source'], r['parent'], r['name'], tuple(r.get('extras') or ()), r.get('specifier'), r.get('url'), bool(r.get('marker_applicable'))) for r in res['requirements'])

r = Requirement('Tool[PDF,Security_Extra] ~=1.4.5 ; python_version >= "3.10" and extra == "security-extra"')
base = [{'name': 'tool', 'version': '1.4.5'}, {'name': 'tool', 'version': '1.4.9'}, {'name': 'tool', 'version': '1.5'}]
variant = [{'name': 'Tool', 'version': '1.5.0'}, {'name': 'tool', 'version': '1.4.9'}, {'name': 'tool', 'version': '1.4.5'}]
env = {'python_version': '3.11'}
a = resolve_metadata([r], base, env, requested_extras={'Security_Extra'})
b = resolve_metadata(['tool[security-extra,pdf] ~= 1.4.5 ; extra == "security_extra" and python_version >= "3.10"'], variant, env, requested_extras={'security-extra'})
print(r.name, sorted(r.extras), str(r.specifier), r.marker.evaluate(env, {'security_extra'}))
print(sorted(a['selected'].items()))
print(a['selected'] == b['selected'] and a['excluded'] == b['excluded'] and a['requested_extras'] == b['requested_extras'])
print(req_fields(a))
print(is_requirement_satisfied(r, a['selected']['tool'], env, {'security-extra'}))
all_cases.expected_output:
tool ['pdf', 'security-extra'] ~=1.4.5 True
[('tool', '1.4.9')]
True
[('root', None, 'tool', ('pdf', 'security-extra'), '~=1.4.5', None, True)]
True
all_cases.actual:
tool ['pdf', 'security-extra'] ~=1.4.5 True
[('tool', '1.4.9')]
True
[('root', None, 'tool', ('pdf', 'security-extra'), '~=1.4.5', None, True)]
True
all_cases.expected:
tool ['pdf', 'security-extra'] ~=1.4.5 True
[('tool', '1.4.9')]
True
[('root', None, 'tool', ('pdf', 'security-extra'), '~=1.4.5', None, True)]
True

all_cases.description: Environment markers change only the applicable dependency closure, and selected, edge, and dependent views agree across fresh environment variants.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import resolve_metadata
variants = [
    (
        [
            {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['backport>=1; python_version < "3.10"', 'modern>=1; python_version >= "3.10"']},
            {'name': 'backport', 'version': '1.0'},
            {'name': 'modern', 'version': '1.0'},
        ],
        'python_version', '3.9', '3.11', 'backport', 'modern',
    ),
    (
        [
            {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['posix-driver>=1; os_name == "posix"', 'nt-driver>=1; os_name == "nt"']},
            {'name': 'posix-driver', 'version': '1.0'},
            {'name': 'nt-driver', 'version': '1.0'},
        ],
        'os_name', 'posix', 'nt', 'posix-driver', 'nt-driver',
    ),
]
for candidates, key, left, right, left_dep, right_dep in variants:
    a = resolve_metadata(['app>=1'], candidates, {key: left})
    b = resolve_metadata(['app>=1'], list(reversed(candidates)), {key: right})
    print(a['selected']['app'], left_dep in a['selected'], right_dep in a['selected'], a['dependents'].get(left_dep) == ['app'])
    print(b['selected']['app'], left_dep in b['selected'], right_dep in b['selected'], b['dependents'].get(right_dep) == ['app'])
all_cases.expected_output:
1 True False True
1 False True True
1 True False True
1 False True True
all_cases.actual:
1 True False True
1 False True True
1 True False True
1 False True True
all_cases.expected:
1 True False True
1 False True True
1 True False True
1 False True True

all_cases.description: Dependency edges, reverse dependents, and requested extras are inverse projections of the same URL, extras, and marker-qualified facts.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import resolve_metadata
candidates = [
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['core>=1', 'docs-renderer[html] @ https://example.test/docs-1.whl ; extra == "docs"']},
    {'name': 'core', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['shared>=1']},
    {'name': 'docs-renderer', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['shared>=1; extra == "html"']},
    {'name': 'shared', 'version': '1.0'},
]
res = resolve_metadata(['App[Docs]>=1'], list(reversed(candidates)))
edges = sorted((e['parent'], e['name'], tuple(e['extras']), bool(e['url'])) for e in res['edges'])
dependents = sorted((name, tuple(parents)) for name, parents in res['dependents'].items())
print(edges)
print(dependents)
print(sorted((k, tuple(v)) for k, v in res['requested_extras'].items()))
print(all(parent in res['dependents'].get(child, []) for parent, child, extras, has_url in edges))

all_cases.description: Root and transitive constraints derive the same selected version, excluded versions, and dependents after permutation through resolve_metadata.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import resolve_metadata
candidates = [
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib<3,!=2.0', 'tool>=1']},
    {'name': 'tool', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['Lib>=2']},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.5'},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '2.0'},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '2.5'},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '3.0'},
]
roots = ['app>=1', 'lib>=1,<4,!=1.5', 'Lib <4, !=1.5, >=1.0']
a = resolve_metadata(roots, candidates)
b = resolve_metadata(list(reversed(roots)), list(reversed(candidates)))
print(sorted(a['selected'].items()))
print(a['excluded']['lib'])
print(a['dependents']['lib'])
print(a['selected'] == b['selected'] and a['excluded'] == b['excluded'] and a['dependents'] == b['dependents'])

all_cases.description: Direct URL requirement round trips preserve parsed fields, marker applicability, extras propagation, and resolver-visible dependency closure without requiring exact whitespace.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, resolve_metadata
root = Requirement('Pkg_B[Z,A] @ https://example.test/pkg-b-1.whl ; python_version >= "3.8"')
again = Requirement(str(root))
candidates = [
    {'name': 'pkg-b', 'version': '1.0.0', 'requires': ['helper>=1; extra == "a"', 'zipper>=1; extra == "z"']},
    {'name': 'helper', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'zipper', 'version': '1.0'},
]
env = {'python_version': '3.11'}
a = resolve_metadata([root], candidates, env)
b = resolve_metadata([again], list(reversed(candidates)), env)
print((again.name, sorted(again.extras), again.url, again.marker.evaluate(env)))
print(sorted(a['selected'].items()))
print(sorted((k, tuple(v)) for k, v in a['requested_extras'].items()))
print(a['selected'] == b['selected'] and a['dependents'] == b['dependents'] and a['requested_extras'] == b['requested_extras'])

all_cases.description: Prerelease policy is identical in direct specifier checks, candidate selection, and edge specifier-match projections.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet, resolve_metadata
candidates = [{'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib>=1']}, {'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.1a1'}]
s = SpecifierSet('>=1')
print(s.contains('1.1a1'))
try:
    resolve_metadata(['app>=1'], candidates)
except ValueError:
    print('no-default-prerelease')
res = resolve_metadata(['app>=1'], list(reversed(candidates)), prereleases=True)
print(sorted(res['selected'].items()))
print(next(e['specifier_matches'] for e in res['edges'] if e['name'] == 'lib'))
print(s.contains('1.1a1', prereleases=True))

all_cases.description: Version-valued markers use shared Version ordering when activating root requirements and dependency edges through resolve_metadata.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker, Version, resolve_metadata
candidates = [
    {'name': 'runtime', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['rc-helper>=1; python_full_version < "3.10"']},
    {'name': 'rc-helper', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'addon', 'version': '1.0'},
]
env = {'python_version': '3.10', 'python_full_version': '3.10.0rc1'}
roots = ['runtime>=1', 'addon>=1; python_full_version < "3.10"']
res = resolve_metadata(roots, candidates, env)
print(Version('3.10.0rc1') < Version('3.10'))
print(Marker('python_full_version < "3.10"').evaluate(env))
print(sorted(res['selected'].items()))
print(sorted((e['parent'], e['name']) for e in res['edges']))

all_cases.description: A failed invalid URL-plus-specifier dependency parse leaves caller inputs and parsed objects unchanged, and later valid metadata resolves normally through resolve_metadata.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import InvalidRequirement, Requirement, resolve_metadata
root = Requirement('app[tools]>=1')
bad = [
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['dep>=1', 'bad @ https://example.test/bad-1.whl >=1 ; python_version >= "3.10"']},
    {'name': 'dep', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'bad', 'version': '1.0'},
]
before = repr(bad)
try:
    resolve_metadata([root], bad, {'python_version': '3.11'})
except InvalidRequirement:
    pass
print(repr(bad) == before)
print((root.name, sorted(root.extras), root.marker is None))
good = [
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['dep>=1', 'bad @ https://example.test/bad-1.whl ; python_version >= "3.10"']},
    {'name': 'dep', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'bad', 'version': '1.0'},
]
res = resolve_metadata([root], list(reversed(good)), {'python_version': '3.11'})
print(sorted(res['selected'].items()))
print(sorted((e['name'], bool(e['url'])) for e in res['edges']))

all_cases.description: Equivalent requirement spellings project to the same selected versions, exclusions, and requested extras independent of root and candidate order.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import resolve_metadata
candidates = [{'name': 'Merge_Lib', 'version': '1.0'}, {'name': 'merge-lib', 'version': '2.0'}, {'name': 'merge-lib', 'version': '2.5'}, {'name': 'merge-lib', 'version': '3.0'}]
roots = ['Merge_Lib[PDF,fast] >= 1.0, <3, !=2.0', 'merge-lib[fast,pdf] < 3.0, != 2, >= 1']
a = resolve_metadata(roots, candidates)
b = resolve_metadata(list(reversed(roots)), list(reversed(candidates)))
print(sorted(a['selected'].items()))
print(a['excluded']['merge-lib'])
print(a['selected'] == b['selected'] and a['excluded'] == b['excluded'] and a['requested_extras'] == b['requested_extras'])

all_cases.description: Parent extras activate URL dependency requirements, and dependency-requested extras activate child optional dependencies in selected, edge, and requested-extra views.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import resolve_metadata
candidates = [
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['renderer[unicode] @ https://example.test/renderer-1.whl ; extra == "pdf"']},
    {'name': 'renderer', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['font[emoji]>=1; extra == "unicode"']},
    {'name': 'font', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['emoji-font>=1; extra == "emoji"']},
    {'name': 'emoji-font', 'version': '1.0'},
]
plain = resolve_metadata(['app>=1'], candidates)
pdf = resolve_metadata(['App[PDF]>=1'], list(reversed(candidates)))
print(sorted(plain['selected'].items()))
print(sorted(pdf['selected'].items()))
print(sorted((e['parent'], e['name'], tuple(e['extras']), bool(e['url'])) for e in pdf['edges']))
print(sorted((k, tuple(v)) for k, v in pdf['requested_extras'].items()))

all_cases.description: Parsed requirement objects can be reused across fresh resolve_metadata environment variants and candidate permutations without retaining prior resolution state.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement, resolve_metadata
root = Requirement('app>=1')
candidates = [
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['linux-only>=1; sys_platform == "linux"', 'win-only>=1; sys_platform == "win32"']},
    {'name': 'linux-only', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'win-only', 'version': '1.0'},
]
linux = resolve_metadata([root], candidates, {'sys_platform': 'linux'})
win = resolve_metadata([root], list(reversed(candidates)), {'sys_platform': 'win32'})
again = resolve_metadata([root], candidates, {'sys_platform': 'linux'})
print(sorted(linux['selected'].items()))
print(sorted(win['selected'].items()))
print(linux == again)
print((root.name, sorted(root.extras), root.marker is None))
